chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: هُ ضَمَّنَ الْقَائِدَ وَ السَّائِقَ وَ الرَّاكِبَ فَقَالَ مَا أَصَابَ الرِّجْلُ فَعَلَى السَّائِقِ وَ مَا أَصَابَ الْيَدُ فَعَلَى الْقَائِدِ وَ الرَّاكِبِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that the guide, and the usher, and the rider is responsible, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whatever the (hind) legs hit, so it is upon the usher, and whatever the hands (front legs) hit, so it is upon the guide and the rider’.
